description: On-demand competitor ad scan. When the user asks what competitors are running, Runneth pulls each tracked competitor's active ads via motion inspo-creatives, summarizes what every brand is running and what changed since the last scan, answers in chat, and refreshes one durable reference file per workspace (/agent/brain/competition/<workspace-slug>/competitor-watch.md) holding the tracked competitor list, the latest scan summary, and scan dates. First run asks the user which competitors to track. Installs a user.md instruction (after the first successful scan) that reads the watch file before answering competitor questions. No scheduled routine, no Slack delivery, no config files — re-run any time, and the user can ask Runneth to schedule a recurring scan themselves.

installs.purpose: The on-demand competitor scan skill — first-run competitor selection in chat, ad pull, diff vs the previous scan recorded in competitor-watch.md, chat report, watch-file refresh, user.md install

runtime_installs.trigger: After the first successful scan for any workspace, idempotent across subsequent runs
runtime_installs.purpose: Standing instruction so competitor questions read the workspace's competitor-watch.md first (tracked list + latest scan summary with dates) and refresh it via the skill when the question needs fresher data

post_install.name: Introduce the new capability
post_install.description: Read post-install-intro.md from this use case folder. After all prior install and post-install steps complete, surface the file's sections to the user in chat as the closing message of the install turn: '## What just opened up' as prose, '## Try this now' as a numbered prompt list, '## Compounds with' only when present and at least one named use case is installed in the sandbox.

creates_at_runtime: /agent/brain/competition/<workspace-slug>/competitor-watch.md (tracked competitor list + last-scan summary + scan dates)

changelog.notes: Trimmed to a single on-demand skill; cut the auto-invoked setup skill, the auto-scheduled weekly routine, auto Slack posts, and baseline state files. The skill now runs only when asked: it pulls each tracked competitor's active ads, reports in chat what each brand is running and what changed since the previous scan, and refreshes one durable file per workspace (/agent/brain/competition/<workspace-slug>/competitor-watch.md). First run asks the user in chat which competitors to track — the tracked list lives in the watch file, replacing inspoBrands.json and its reminderId/slackChannelId/schedule state. Survival-cohort analysis and per-brand baseline JSONs replaced by a plain new/gone diff against the previous scan summary. migration-helper.sh removed. user.md snippet runneth-apps:competitor-intel:read-before-competitor-questions v2 installed after the first successful scan, idempotent via sentinel. The skill may tip the user that they can ask Runneth for a recurring scan, but never creates one itself.

Path migration. Brain data write path moves from /agent/brain/competitor-intel/<workspace-slug>/ to /agent/brain/competition/<workspace-slug>/ so competitor watch output sits inside the Competition brain domain instead of a sibling root. Aligns with brain-onboard's ten-domain layout. The skill install path stays at /agent/.agents/skills/competitor-intel/ — only the brain data path moves. Includes migration-helper.sh that detects /agent/brain/competitor-intel/ on existing installs, moves contents to /agent/brain/competition/ preserving accumulated weekly snapshots and baselines, and leaves a 30-day stub at the old path. No behavior changes to the weekly run itself.
Added post-install intro. After install completes, Runneth surfaces a short 'what just opened up + try this now' message sourced from post-install-intro.md.

Initial release. Translated from motion-creative-plugin competitor-intel MCP skill. Competitor brands tracked in workspace-scoped inspoBrands.json seed. MCP tools replaced with native motion CLI. Two-pass active portfolio pull, survival cohort analysis, weekly delta computation. Setup skill handles brand resolution, own-brand ID storage, Slack channel config, and reminder scheduling.
